query: What tasks are currently in WIP status?
answer:
Here are the tasks currently in `WIP` status:

*   **QRD-4: Code recordings into themes (Dovetail)** is in **Q2 Roadmap · demo-evalfixt** and due on `2026-06-03` [task:160].
*   **QRD-2: Synthesize 12 customer interviews into themes** is in **Q2 Roadmap · demo-evalfixt** and due on `2026-06-05` [task:158].
*   **WRD-8: Investigate slow homepage load on mobile Safari** is in **Website Redesign · demo-evalfixt** and due on `2026-05-29` [task:155].
*   **WRD-5: Implement responsive navigation with mobile drawer** is in **Website Redesign · demo-evalfixt** and due on `2026-06-03` [task:152].
*   **WRD-4: Build Hero + FeatureGrid in design system v3** is in **Website Redesign · demo-evalfixt** and due on `2026-05-31` [task:151].

Sprint 1 kickoff recap
✅ Decisions
Hero illustration: ship the gradient variant. Reasons: better perf, more A/B flexibility for the v1.1 copy test.
Proactive surfacing vs search: decision deferred. Revisit after ≥ 8 customer interview transcripts are coded (target: next Friday).
Animation framework for marketing pages: CSS-only. framer-motion ruled out on bundle-size grounds.
“What's new” launch modal: yes. Will highlight Cmd-K and the redesign. Carol owns the illustration, Bob wires the modal.
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Roadmap proposal v1 — top 5 bets for Q3
🧾 Summary Write the v1 roadmap proposal: five concrete bets for Q3 with an expected outcome, evidence link, and rough effort estimate. Optimized for leadership review and prioritization debate. ✅ Acceptance criteria Five bets, each ≤ 300 words. Each bet cites at least one interview quote AND one competitor data point. Rough sizing (S / M / L / XL) and lead-team identified per bet. Reviewed with leadership in a 60-minute working session. 🎯 Notes & links Top candidate bets so far: (1) proactive surfacing assistant, (2) sprint-board polish based on Linear teardown, (3) onboarding overhaul, (4) admin / SSO maturity for enterprise, (5) public API. Order will change as synthesis lands.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Synthesis target: Thursday. Should give you the long weekend to draft.
</workspace_content>

matches.title: Q2 Discovery Sprint
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
Q2 Discovery Sprint 🎯 Goal Close Q2's discovery cycle with a roadmap proposal grounded in customer evidence: 12 interview transcripts coded into themes, a competitive teardown of Notion / Slack / Linear, and a top-5 bets recommendation for Q3. ✅ Success criteria 12 customer...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Q2 Discovery Sprint
🎯 Goal Close Q2's discovery cycle with a roadmap proposal grounded in customer evidence: 12 interview transcripts coded into themes, a competitive teardown of Notion / Slack / Linear, and a top-5 bets recommendation for Q3. ✅ Success criteria 12 customer interview transcripts coded into ≤ 8 themes in Dovetail. Competitor analysis covers feature, pricing, and onboarding for each of Notion, Slack, Linear. Roadmap proposal document reviewed by leadership and lands within the Q2 deadline. Each bet in the Q3 proposal cites at least one interview quote or competitor data point. 📦 In scope Customer interview synthesis (theme coding + cluster summaries). Competitor product teardown (3 competitors, 3 surfaces each). Top-5 Q3 bets document with effort estimates. 🚫 Out of scope Pricing experimentation (separate workstream). Engineering capacity planning (handled by EM team). ⚠️ Risks & dependencies Interview coverage is uneven — only 7 of 12 segments are enterprise users. If the synthesis reveals enterprise-heavy themes we should be cautious about extrapolating to SMB without a follow-up round.
</workspace_content>

matches.title: Customer interview synthesis methodology
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
📝 Common pitfalls — Collapsing themes too early — “notifications + search” is two themes, not one, until the evidence proves otherwise. Cherry-picking quotes — every theme needs a count, not just a representative quote. Recency bias — coding the latest interviews first inflates...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Customer interview synthesis methodology
📝 Common pitfalls
Collapsing themes too early — “notifications + search” is two themes, not one, until the evidence proves otherwise.
Cherry-picking quotes — every theme needs a count, not just a representative quote.
Recency bias — coding the latest interviews first inflates their weight. Code in interview order.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Customer interview synthesis methodology
📚 Customer interview synthesis — methodology
Documenting the approach so future synthesis rounds reproduce it. Took two iterations last quarter to land on this; the cost is upfront but the output quality is meaningfully higher.
</workspace_content>

answer:
Based on recent activity, here’s a summary of what’s happening:

Sprint 1 is underway for two main efforts: the **Website Redesign** [chat:pm:15] and the **Q2 Roadmap** [chat:pm:16]. A recap of the sprint kickoff highlights the key decisions, blockers, and next steps [note:chat:9].

### Key Decisions
*   **Hero illustration:** The gradient variant was chosen for the website redesign for better performance and A/B testing flexibility [note:chat:9].
*   **Animation framework:** The team has decided to use CSS-only for marketing page animations, ruling out `framer-motion` due to its bundle size [note:chat:9].
*   **"What's new" modal:** A launch modal will be built to highlight the redesign and new Cmd-K functionality [note:chat:9].

### Blockers & Risks
*   The **roadmap proposal** is blocked pending the synthesis of customer interviews, which is on the critical path. A decision on whether to prioritize proactive surfacing or improved search for Q3 hinges on this synthesis [note:chat:9, chat:gm:8].
*   The **accessibility audit** for the website redesign is blocked until the `FeatureGrid` PR lands [note:chat:9].
*   The design for the **pricing-table component** is gated on receiving final tier copy from the marketing team [note:chat:9, chat:dm:30].

### In Progress
*   For the **Website Redesign**, a pull request for the responsive navigation is up for review [chat:gm:8].
*   For the **Q2 Roadmap**, 5 of 12 customer interviews still need to be scheduled. The synthesis of the completed interviews is ongoing [chat:pm:16].

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Sprint 1 kickoff recap
🚧 Blockers and risks
Dan can't start the a11y audit until the FeatureGrid PR lands. Watch for delay.
Synthesis is on the critical path for the roadmap proposal. If it slips past end of Sprint 1, the proposal slips too.
Pricing component design is gated on marketing's tier-copy confirmation by Thursday.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Sprint 1 kickoff recap
📅 Next checkpoints
Friday — End-of-week demo. About-page migration walkthrough.
Next Friday — Decision on proactive-surfacing vs search (with 8+ transcripts coded).
End of Sprint 1 — Marketing PR train and the Plausible event PR both landed.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Sprint 1 kickoff recap
✅ Decisions
Hero illustration: ship the gradient variant. Reasons: better perf, more A/B flexibility for the v1.1 copy test.
Proactive surfacing vs search: decision deferred. Revisit after ≥ 8 customer interview transcripts are coded (target: next Friday).
Animation framework for marketing pages: CSS-only. framer-motion ruled out on bundle-size grounds.
“What's new” launch modal: yes. Will highlight Cmd-K and the redesign. Carol owns the illustration, Bob wires the modal.
</workspace_content>

matches.title: Alice Chen
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
Whether the Q3 roadmap should bet on proactive surfacing (assistant tells you what you missed) or just better search. Interview signal is leaning proactive but we want more transcripts coded first.
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:

Whether the Q3 roadmap should bet on proactive surfacing (assistant tells you what you missed) or just better search. Interview signal is leaning proactive but we want more transcripts coded first.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Previously:
Whether the Q3 roadmap should bet on proactive surfacing (assistant tells you what you missed) or just better search. Interview signal is leaning proactive but we want more transcripts coded first.
When you say “proactive surfacing,” are we talking about a daily digest, or something that interrupts in-stream?

Message:
Closer to a daily digest, I think. The interview phrasing is “Monday morning catchup,” not “interrupt me while I'm focused.”
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Whether the Q3 roadmap should bet on proactive surfacing (assistant tells you what you missed) or just better search. Interview signal is leaning proactive but we want more transcripts coded first.
Whether the Q3 roadmap should bet on proactive surfacing (assistant tells you what you missed) or just better search. Interview signal is leaning proactive but we want more transcripts coded first.
When you say “proactive surfacing,” are we talking about a daily digest, or something that interrupts in-stream?
Closer to a daily digest, I think. The interview phrasing is “Monday morning catchup,” not “interrupt me while I'm focused.”
OK that's much easier to scope. Notification fatigue concerns get a lot smaller.
Right — and it dovetails with the spotlight Cmd-K we're already shipping. Same underlying retrieval, different surface.
</workspace_content>
